news.title: RBI bars banks from offering NDF contracts to corporates to stabilise rupee
news.india_relevance: The RBI is intervening in the offshore rupee market to control currency volatility and speculation.
news.why_it_matters:
By restricting non-deliverable forwards, the RBI aims to reduce the influence of offshore speculators on the domestic rupee value.
This move forces corporates to hedge through onshore channels, increasing regulatory oversight of currency flows.

news.title: India restricts imports of gold, silver and platinum jewellery to curb FTA misuse
news.india_relevance: The government is closing loopholes in Free Trade Agreements that allowed duty-free bullion imports.
news.why_it_matters:
This policy shift targets the surge in imports from countries like Indonesia and UAE that bypassed standard customs duties.
It protects domestic refiners and helps manage the Current Account Deficit by tightening bullion trade rules.

news.title: India's top consulting and audit firms hit by AI and war; job cuts, hiring freeze loom
news.india_relevance: The 'Big Four' and major consulting firms in India are facing a structural slowdown in demand.
news.why_it_matters:
Generative AI is automating entry-level audit and consulting tasks, while global geopolitical uncertainty has paused discretionary spending.
This signals a major shift in the white-collar job market for India's top-tier graduates.

news.title: USTR lists India's UPI and data rules as trade barriers
news.india_relevance: The US Trade Representative has flagged India's digital public infrastructure and data localization as protectionist.
news.why_it_matters:
This creates friction in Indo-US trade relations, specifically regarding the dominance of UPI over global card networks like Visa/Mastercard.
It highlights the growing global tension between national digital sovereignty and international trade norms.

news.title: Banks, telecom companies to chuck OTPs, adopt silent authentication
news.india_relevance: Indian financial institutions are moving away from SMS-based 2FA to combat rising phishing and SIM-swap fraud.
news.why_it_matters:
Silent authentication uses SIM-based verification in the background, removing the friction of manual OTP entry.
This is a major structural shift in India's digital security architecture for millions of mobile banking users.

news.title: Venezuelan Oil Exports Hit 6-Year High as India Fills China Gap - Bloomberg.com
news.india_relevance: Indian refiners are aggressively sourcing heavy crude from Venezuela following the easing of US sanctions.
news.why_it_matters:
India is diversifying its energy basket away from Russia and the Middle East to secure cheaper feedstock.
This shift impacts India's trade balance and the margins of large private and public sector refiners.

news.title: Trai loses oversight over TV ratings, govt takes full control
news.india_relevance: The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting is centralizing the governance of television audience measurement.
news.why_it_matters:
Removing TRAI's oversight ends a long-standing regulatory tug-of-war over how TV ad spends are targeted.
This could lead to a complete overhaul of the BARC rating system, impacting the ₹30,000 crore TV advertising industry.

news.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis: 23 ships attacked, 1,900 stranded, traffic falls from 130 to 6: Navy Chief
news.india_relevance: The disruption of this critical maritime chokepoint directly threatens India's energy security and export routes.
news.why_it_matters:
